Title: PIN2-mediated self-organizing transient auxin flow contributes to auxin maxima at the tip of Arabidopsis cotyledons

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coordinates pavement cell interdigitation and cotyledon morphogenesis via auxin maxima at the cotyledon tip
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly specific accumulation in cotyledon marginal cells during early seedling growth; tonoplast localization increases around 60–72 HAP, progressing from tip to base
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in epidermal cells in shoots; highly specific in cotyledon marginal cells
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Tonoplast/vacuolar membrane; also reticular/prevacuolar pattern earlier
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TOB1 downregulates PIN2 levels by vacuolar sequestration of IBA, thereby reducing IBA-derived IAA and terminating transient auxin flow; loss of TOB1 increases PIN2, enhances DR5 auxin response, and enlarges cotyledons
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants (tob1-1, tob1-3); introgression with PIN2-GFP and DR5::GUS reporters; confocal colocalization with tonoplast markers CFP-Vac-ck, FM4-64, BCECF; exogenous IAA treatment; fluorometric GUS assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous IAA treatment (100 nM, 4 h) increased TOB1 accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TOB1 accumulates in cotyledon marginal cells and shifts to tonoplast localization as PIN2 disappears. 2. TOB1 accumulation pattern is inversely correlated with PIN2 along the cotyledon margin. 3. tob1 mutants show increased PIN2 levels and enhanced DR5::GUS auxin response at cotyledon tip and edges. 4. Exogenous IAA induces TOB1 accumulation, supporting auxin-triggered negative feedback on auxin flow.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TOB1 regulation of PIN2 is cotyledon-specific; in roots, TOB1 and PIN2 are not accumulated in the same cell type and tob1 does not alter root PIN2 levels or gravitropism
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of TOB1 increases cotyledon surface area and epidermal cell expansion, indicating altered developmental coordination

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Paper states ECH2 is a limiting step in IBA-to-IAA conversion
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: IAA precursor/intermediate contributing to IAA formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a peroxisomal β-oxidation step required for conversion of indole-3-butyric acid (IBA) into indole-3-acetic acid (IAA)
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies local IAA pool by converting IBA to IAA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cotyledon tip auxin maxima and coordinates pavement cell interdigitation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cotyledon tip; increased auxin along marginal cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: YFP-ECH2 accumulates in cotyledon marginal cells before PIN2 establishment and later also at cotyledon tip
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed at this developmental stage according to transcript data
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ECH2-dependent IBA-to-IAA conversion promotes onset of PIN2 accumulation in marginal cells, thereby supporting auxin transport, cotyledon tip auxin maxima, and pavement cell interdigitation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Translational reporter (ECH2p::YFP-ECH2); mutant analysis of ech2ibr10; PIN2-GFP quantification; pavement cell morphometry
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental time course; mutant timed by hours after root protrusion due to delayed germination
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ECH2 accumulates in marginal cells before PIN2 appears. 2. ech2ibr10 mutants show reduced apical PIN2-GFP in cotyledon marginal cells. 3. ech2ibr10 cotyledons are smaller, hyponastic, and have impaired pavement cell interdigitation. 4. Results support IBA-derived auxin as the main trigger for PIN2-mediated auxin flow.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IPyA pathway genes are weakly expressed before PIN2 establishment; ECH2-supported IBA pathway predominates at onset of the process

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Auxin efflux transporter establishing polar auxin transport
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forms auxin maxima at cotyledon tip that globally coordinate pavement cell interdigitation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Polar auxin transport and local auxin maximum formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary transporter forming a transient tip-ward auxin flow along marginal cells that contributes decisively to cotyledon tip auxin maximum
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly expressed in early imbibed seeds; specifically accumulates transiently in cotyledon marginal cells from ~24–72 HAP, peaking before pavement cell interdigitation; apically polarized toward cotyledon tip
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Among PINs, strongest early expression in imbibed seeds
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Apical plasma membrane of cotyledon marginal cells
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Auxin distribution/IAA accumulation at cotyledon tip; non-cell-autonomous promotion of pavement cell interdigitation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Polar auxin efflux/transport toward cotyledon tip
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: IBA-derived IAA positively promotes PIN2 accumulation and polarization; exogenous IAA rapidly increases apical PIN2; TOB1-mediated IBA sequestration reduces IAA and downregulates PIN2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback: auxin promotes PIN2 accumulation/polarization. Negative feedback: auxin induces TOB1, which reduces IBA-derived IAA and terminates PIN2 accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants (eir1-1/pin2, pin2-T); PIN2-GFP reporter imaging; DR5::GUS histochemical and fluorometric assays; R2D2 auxin reporter; NPA and IAA treatments; laser ablation of marginal cells
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IAA treatment increases PIN2 within 0.5 h; NPA disrupts PIN2 polarity and pavement cell interdigitation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIN2 promotes transient auxin flow along marginal cells toward the cotyledon tip, increasing tip auxin maximum and enabling coordinated pavement cell interdigitation; pin2 mutants show reduced tip auxin response and reduced interdigitation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIN2 accumulates apically in a contiguous file of cotyledon marginal cells, suggesting tip-ward auxin flow. 2. PIN2 accumulation is transient and precedes pavement cell interdigitation. 3. pin2 mutants show reduced DR5 auxin response at cotyledon tip and reduced pavement cell interdigitation. 4. Auxin treatment rapidly enhances apical PIN2 accumulation, whereas transport inhibition disrupts PIN2 polarity. 5. PIN2 operates in a self-organizing transient auxin transport system controlled by positive and negative feedback.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although other PINs may contribute, PIN2 is the core experimentally demonstrated transporter in Results; its function in cotyledons differs from roots in the context of TOB1-mediated regulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of PIN2 reduces pavement cell interdigitation and cell size at the cotyledon tip, impairing normal cotyledon morphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Published transcriptional data and Arabidopsis ePlant browser/translatome datasets were used; accession numbers not provided in the paper
